source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Incident

event.top_event: Personnel basket struck vessel railing and platform structure during transfer
actions_taken: Crane immediately taken out of service
All personnel sent for medical evaluation
Hydraulic swing gear motor removed, rebuilt, reinstalled and tested by Sparrows Crane Mechanics
Swing gear motor replaced with 10 bolt pattern installation
Gulf wide alert initiated by Energy XXI
Inspection process revised with specific details on bolt types, torque values and swing drive bolt condition
event.summary: During personnel transfer from M/V Patience Lab to SP 93 B Platform using a 4-Man Billy Pugh basket, a sudden wind gust caused the crane boom to swing. The crane operator attempted to lock the swing brake but it failed to hold. The hydraulic swing gear motor (slew drive system) had experienced structural failure due to mounting bolts backing out, resulting in complete loss of boom swing control. The basket struck the vessel railing and platform structure, injuring three personnel. The fourth rider was suspended in the basket for approximately 15 minutes before being lowered back to the vessel.
recommendations: Replace 4 bolt swing gear motor installation with 10 bolt pattern for greater stability and security
Revise annual and quarterly inspection procedures to include specific questioning of bolt types, torque values and condition of swing drive bolts
Implement industry-wide inspection standards for swing gear housing bolts across all platforms
Establish written process for third party crane inspection contractors addressing swing gear housing bolts
Ensure all bolts on swing gear drive housing are accessible and visible through design improvements
key_phrases: structural failure of swing gear drive housing
complete lack of control of boom swing movement

hazards.name: Crane boom swing during personnel transfer
Hydraulic swing gear motor structural failure

threats.name: Loss of swing brake control due to hydraulic motor failure
Mounting bolts backing out of slew drive system
Wind gust causing boom deflection during personnel lift
Undetected deterioration of swing gear housing bolts

consequences.name: Personnel basket impact with vessel railing
Personnel basket impact with platform structure

controls.name: Swing brake system
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical control for boom swing

supporting_text: The crane operator attempted to lock the swing brake, but it would not hold.
He then attempted to raise the personnel basket, while swinging the boom in the opposite direction, so it would not hit the railing.

controls.name: Hydraulic swing gear motor (slew drive system)
controls.lod_basis: Primary hydraulic system for boom control

supporting_text: The BSEE investigation determined that the crane's hydraulic swing gear motor (slew drive system) had failed.

The mounting bolts that secured it to the cab base had backed out and resulted in a structural failure of the swing gear drive housing, which made the motor insecure and allowed it to 'cock sideways' when operated and prevented any control of swing movement.

controls.name: Daily pre-use crane inspection
controls.lod_basis: Routine inspection to identify mechanical failures

supporting_text: An inspection of the bolts is not part of the normal daily pre-use, quarterly, or annual inspection.
To detect abnormal movement of the hydraulic motor requires two personnel, a crane operator and an observer, while in operation.

controls.name: Quarterly crane inspection
controls.lod_basis: Periodic inspection to identify developing failures

supporting_text: The last quarterly inspection prior to the incident was 11/10/2016.
An inspection of the bolts is not part of the normal daily pre-use, quarterly, or annual inspection.

controls.name: Annual crane inspection
controls.lod_basis: Annual certification to ensure equipment fitness

supporting_text: The last annual crane inspection prior to the incident was 2/16/2016.
Third party crane inspection contractor had no written process addressing the swing gear housing bolts.

controls.name: Third party crane inspection contractor procedures
controls.barrier_role: Ensure standardized inspection protocols are followed
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control for inspection quality assurance

supporting_text: Third party crane inspection contractor had no written process addressing the swing gear housing bolts.

controls.name: Boom angle selection for personnel lift
controls.barrier_role: Minimize susceptibility to wind effects during lift
controls.lod_basis: Operational planning to reduce environmental hazard exposure

supporting_text: The boom angle used during the lift made it susceptible to be affected by gusting winds.

controls.name: Personnel emergency response and rescue
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity and duration of personnel exposure
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to mitigate consequences of incident

supporting_text: Three of the riders managed to jump off as the basket swung and hit the boat railing, while the fourth rider held on as it crashed into the bullwork and then lifted up over the railing and swung over and hit the platform.
Personnel were able to get him off the basket about 15 minutes later after the vessel maneuvered under the basket and the crane operator lowered the basket back down onto the boat.
The crane operator at SP 93 B as well as the deckhand are to be commended for their actions to prevent greater injuries to personnel once the slew drive system failed.

controls.name: Medical evaluation and treatment
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through prompt medical intervention

supporting_text: The injured, as well as the entire construction crew on the boat, were sent in for medical evaluation.
The decision was made to send the M/V to the GI Shore Base 3-1/2 hrs away with the entire crew on board.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
